title: 第 208 節
en: Be propitiation, with sacrifice, prayer, propitiation, and glorification.
Yathft ahft vairyd: The wilt of the Lord is the law of holiness ....
I. We sacrifice unto Verethraghna, made by Ahura.
Zarathurtra asked Ahura Mazda: 'Ahura Mazda, most beneficent Spirit, Maker of the material world, thou Holy One !
^ Sirdzah I, ao.
232 YA5TS AND skdZAHS.
Ahura Mazda answered : * It is Verethraghna, made by Ahuia, O Spitama Zarathortral'

2. Verethraghna, made by Ahura, came to him first, running in the shape of a strong, beautiful wind, made by Mazda; he bore the good Glory, made by Mazda, the Glory made by Mazda, that is both health and strength.

3. Then he, who is the strongest \ said unto him*: torious in victory ; I am the most glorious in Glory ; I am the most favouring in favour ; I am the best giver of welfare ; I am the best-healing in healthgiving.
4. 'And I shall destroy the malice of all the malicious, the malice of Da^vas and men, of the Ydtus and Pairikas, of the oppressors, the blind, and the deaf.

5. * For his brightness and glory, I will offer unto him a sacrifice worth being heard; namely, unto Verethraghna, made by Ahura. We worship Verethraghna, made by Ahura, with an offering of libations, according to the primitive ordinances of Ahura; with the Haoma and meat, the baresma, the wisdom of the tongue, the holy spells, the speech, the deeds, the libations, and the rightlyspoken words.
6. *We sacrifice unto Verethraghna, made by Ahura/ ^ ' Verethraghna. * Zarathuxtra.
bahrAm vast. 233 Zarathiutra asked Ahura Mazda : ' Ahura Mazda, most beneficent Spiriti Maker of the material world, thou Holy One !
Ahura Mazda answered : * It is Verethraghna, made by Ahufa, O Spitama Zarathunra!'

7. Verethraghna, made by Ahura, came to him the second time, running in the shape of a beautiful bull, with yellow ears and golden horns ; upon whose horns floated the well-shapen Strength, and Victory, beautiful of form, made by Ahura: thus did he come, bearing the good Glory, made by Mazda, the Glory made by Mazda, that is both health and strength.
Then he, who is the strongest, said unto him : ' I am the strongest in strength . . . .\ For his brightness and glory, I wiH offer unto him a sacrifice worth being heard .... * III.

Zarathuitra asked Ahura Mazda : 'Ahura Mazda, most beneficent Spirit, Maker of the material world, thou Holy One !

and Victory, beautiful of form, made by Ahura : thus did he come, bearing the good Glory, made by Mazda, that is both health and strength.
Then he, who is the strongest, said unto him : ' I am the strongest in strength ....

For his brightness and glory, I will offer unto him a sacrifice worth being heard ....

Ahura Mazda answered : * It is Verethraghna, made by Ahura, O Spitama Zarathu^ral'

11. Verethraghna, made by Ahura, came to him the fourth time, running in the shape of a burdenbearing^ camel, sharp-toothed*, swift . . , . ■, stamping forwards, long-haired, and living in the abodes of men * ;

1 2. Who of all males in rut shows greatest strength and greatest fire, when he goes to his females. Of all females those are best kept whom a burdenbearing camel keeps, who has thick forelegs and large humps, ....•, quick-eyed, long-headed, bright, tall, and strong;
